creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
videos.summary: Nishant Gupta from Meta SuperIntelligence Labs presents evaluation frameworks for autonomous AI agents in production. The talk covers limitations of offline benchmarks, continuous evaluation pipelines, detection of drift and hallucinations, and metrics for multi-step agentic workflows.

videos.title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
videos.summary: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents shift software engineering work from line-by-line code writing to system-level design. She contends that core engineering disciplines like systems thinking, decomposition, and state management remain essential, with engineering value moving to the architectural layer rather than disappearing.

videos.title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
videos.summary: Jacob E. Thomas presents the Miranda Hypothesis, which identifies how cultural representation density in training data causes persona-based AI systems to output smoothed cultural composites rather than reasoning from primary sources. He proposes a shift from cognitive simulation benchmarks to epistemic simulation with temporally-anchored, expert-evaluated rubrics that detect anachronistic reasoning and documentary inconsistencies that automated metrics miss.

videos.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 3 Livestream
videos.summary: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 held its final day of main-stage programming on July 2, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. The livestream featured keynotes, talks, and a startup battlefield competition, serving as a major industry gathering point.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin reviews Google's 50-page whitepaper on agentic engineering, arguing that model selection contributes only 10% to AI coding success while the harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) constitutes the other 90%. The video breaks down the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering and contends that context engineering and orchestration skills are the key differentiators for 2026.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code is an open-source, coding-focused agentic model developed by Moonshot AI. The model has been noted for strong performance relative to its price and size.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Best AI engineers use autonomous loops that prompt agents repeatedly without manual intervention, with orchestrator agents spinning up parallel worker agents for tasks. This approach reduces manual prompting but increases token costs and requires observability infrastructure to manage hallucinations reliably.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding models as interchangeable workers, enabling users to route tasks to different providers like Claude Code, Codex, and Pi based on the job at hand. It provides governance, human-in-the-loop controls, and cross-device session management for agentic coding workflows.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason announces a new secret project from the OpenClaw creator involving a skills repository for Crabbox and codebase harness setup. The announcement includes links to a GitHub repo, an AI Builder Club community, and a Superdesign tool.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: Loop Engineer is a technique for designing and building AI agents. The video provides setup guidance and a knowledge template via GitHub, with additional workshops available through AI Builder Club.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video tutorial on constructing proactive AI agents that self-improve through feedback loops. Explains autonomous optimization for ads and SEO using tools like Loopany, memory systems, and agent tools to enable continuous improvement without human intervention.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason predicts the emergence of real autonomous coding agents, discussing Ralph-loop 2.0 and the /goal feature as examples of multi-step task automation. The video covers goal-buddy and /mission exploration for long-horizon coding tasks.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: GPT explained visually..
videos.summary: A visual walkthrough of how Generative Pretrained Transformers work, covering self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections. The creator explains that GPT powers models like Gemini, Opus, Kimi, Nemotron, and Qwen, and that understanding these mechanics is accessible.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code presents a 10-minute walkthrough of Andrej Karpathy's bigram language model, covering tokenization, embedding, training, and loss calculation. The video serves as a foundation for understanding larger models like GPT, demonstrating core techniques like cross-entropy loss, softmax, and the AdamW optimizer.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 in Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The architecture combines hybrid mamba transformer, Latent Mixture of Experts, and MTP into a unified design that differentiates it from other open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, an LLM that shifts from full attention to sparse attention (MSA) using tiled I/O and optimized KV cache reads. The design reduces memory bandwidth bottlenecks during prefill and decode stages of inference.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: Tool calling enables AI agents to request and use external tools rather than relying solely on training data. The technique involves the model generating structured requests, a framework executing the tool, and looping to incorporate results. This transforms agents from guessing chatbots into systems that can access real-time data and take actions.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS is a tool that equips AI coding agents with up-to-date AWS documentation, best practices, and tested procedures. It improves agent success rates and reduces wasted effort by providing reliable knowledge instead of relying on outdated general knowledge.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers demonstrate how to run Claude Code, an agentic coding tool by Anthropic, on Amazon Bedrock while keeping data within AWS infrastructure. The walkthrough covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, team cost tracking, and Bedrock Guardrails for content governance.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses job market strategies for 2026 amid AI adoption, emphasizing networking, personal branding, and practical skill-building over resume mass-application. The video argues that junior developers remain valuable despite AI capabilities and that job evolution rather than replacement is the historical pattern.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A YouTube video comparing code generation practices, particularly contrasting OpenAI's Codex and Anthropic's Claude with Google's internal code generation approaches across multiple years. The content examines whether Google engineers adopt informal or rapid coding practices alongside formal development.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech released a video discussing Malus, a company or product positioned as transformative or disruptive to open source development. The video covers what open source is, an AI agent example, how Malus operates, and implications for open source maintainers.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that software competitive advantage comes from customer context, proprietary data, and operational complexity rather than code alone. He discusses how Gong built a meeting assistant product, the importance of creating raving fans, and argues that fears of AI disrupting SaaS are exaggerated.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a "ChatGPT Moment" is coming for robotics but warns against myopic focus on humanoid robots led by companies like 1X and Unitree. She contends that designing robots for human interaction and delight, rather than utility alone, will be critical as billions of robots enter everyday life.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Pigment is an AI performance management platform that integrates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data into a single workspace for decision-making. Founded by Eléonore Crespo, it has raised over $400 million and serves enterprise customers including Figma, Brex, and Anthropic.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that rabid users alone do not validate product-market fit and that real PMF manifests as customer pull rather than founder push. He advocates for embedded customer relationships over formal interviews and emphasizes betting on AI model trajectory rather than current capabilities.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Could Delete EVERYTHING! Businesses Ignored This! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that businesses are overlooking critical security vulnerabilities in agentic AI systems, including prompt injection attacks, accidental data deletion, and unauthorized actions. He contends that these risks create justified business hesitation despite AI's potential benefits.

videos.title: AI Podcast: Anthropic's Latest & Lisbon's Electric Vibe #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof's "This Is Tech Year" podcast features discussion of Anthropic's latest developments alongside guest Joao. The episode blends tech news coverage with event announcements including the Santos festival in Lisbon.

videos.title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
videos.summary: Small agile teams are more productive and efficient than large teams, which suffer from excessive meetings and coordination overhead. The video argues that efficiency improves as team size decreases.

videos.title: AI Memory Boom: Micron & SK Hynix Surge! Huge Growth Ahead! #shorts
videos.summary: A short-form video discussing how AI demand is driving growth in high-bandwidth memory production. It highlights Micron and SK Hynix as key players shaping the future memory stack for AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
